nodes.context: White supremacist organization founded in San Francisco in 1984.
British far-right political party that the American Front was modeled after.
White supremacist organization led by Tom Metzger, allied with American Front.
Neo-Nazi organization in England with ties to American Front's magazine.

edges.evidence_quote: It began as a loose organization modeled after the British National Front.
edges.context: American Front was modeled after the British National Front.

edges.evidence_quote: Heick began working with Tom Metzger 's White Aryan Resistance (WAR) in 1988.
edges.context: American Front cooperated with White Aryan Resistance starting in 1988.

edges.evidence_quote: Tension between AF and WAR increased soon after.
edges.context: Tension and disagreement arose between American Front and White Aryan Resistance after Aryan Woodstock.

edges.evidence_quote: the Washington and California AF sections published The Voice of Revolution magazine, which had ties to Combat 18 in England.
edges.context: American Front's magazine had ties to Combat 18.
